object_under_evaluation: tool-use trajectory discipline in bounded agent change workflows
claim_boundary: This report speaks only to whether tool use stayed disciplined and reviewable on this bounded path-sensitive case family, not to overall workflow strength.
limitations: It does not replace the broader outcome-versus-path split surface.
It does not prove that one exact tool sequence is required.

per_case_breakdown.why_path_matters: The task needs a bounded search before editing and one visible verification step after the change.
per_case_breakdown.omission_or_churn_note: The path stayed narrow and skipped decorative exploration.
per_case_breakdown.failure_vs_readout: The readout matches the disciplined tool evidence instead of inflating it into a generic workflow success story.

per_case_breakdown.why_path_matters: Skipping the obvious verification command would leave the change hard to review.
per_case_breakdown.omission_or_churn_note: The agent added extra exploratory commands and nearly lost the visible reason the verification step mattered.
per_case_breakdown.failure_vs_readout: The failure is unnecessary churn around a path-sensitive check, and the readout keeps both the omission risk and the churn visible.

per_case_breakdown.why_path_matters: The same outcome could be reached through a disciplined or noisy path, so proportionality is the real bounded question.
per_case_breakdown.omission_or_churn_note: Extra tool churn was reframed as helpful thoroughness even though it did not add reviewable evidence.
per_case_breakdown.failure_vs_readout: A clean final outcome did not repair the decorative path theater, and the readout had to stay weaker than that visible mismatch.
